name: La'ie Point State Wayside
tagline: A dramatic windswept cliff with views of a natural sea arch and offshore bird sanctuary — free, quick, and totally uncrowded.
parking: Only about 10 spots at the end of Naupaka St — park further down and walk if full. Be respectful of residents
bestTime: Late afternoon for golden light on the cliffs. Morning for calmer seas and better visibility of Kukuihoolua Island
talkStory: A 5-minute walk from the parking area to a rocky coastal point with views of Kukuihoolua Island (the one with the natural arch punched through it) and a seabird sanctuary. Wear closed-toe shoes — the lava rock is sharp and uneven. Not a swimming spot (strong currents and dangerous rocks). No facilities or restrooms. It's a 15-minute stop that pairs perfectly with a North Shore day trip — between PCC and Kahuku shrimp trucks. One of the most photographed spots on the Windward Coast but rarely crowded because most tourists don't know about it.
